senses.definition: Relating to the mathematical ideas or geometry developed by the German mathematician Bernhard Riemann, especially non-Euclidean geometry.
examples: Einstein’s theory of general relativity is based on Riemannian geometry.
In this course we study Riemannian manifolds and their curvature.
The paper introduces a new method in Riemannian analysis.

senses.definition: In South Africa, a narrow strip of leather used to form the seat or back of a chair by weaving it into a lattice.
examples: The old farmhouse still had chairs with riempie seats.
He repaired the broken riempie in the dining chair.
Traditional riempie furniture is highly valued by collectors.

senses.definition: In South Africa, to make or repair a chair or bench by weaving it with leather thongs to form a seat or back.
examples: They learned how to riempie a simple wooden chair.
The craftsman riempied the bench using strong rawhide.
She plans to riempie the old frame instead of buying a new seat.

senses.definition: Plural of riempie: narrow leather strips used in South Africa to weave the seat or back of furniture.
examples: The riempies on the old chair had stretched and needed replacing.
He bought new riempies to restore the antique bench.
The pattern of the riempies gives the chair its unique look.

senses.definition: Plural of riem: in South Africa, strong strips of rawhide used as ropes or thongs for tying and fastening.
examples: The farmer kept several riems hanging in the shed.
They used riems to secure the load on the wagon.
The oxen were held in place with thick leather riems.

senses.definition: A white wine, usually light and fruity, made from Riesling grapes, especially in Germany and nearby regions.
examples: She ordered a glass of dry Riesling with her fish.
The winery is famous for its fruity Riesling.
Riesling pairs well with spicy Asian dishes.

senses.definition: Different types or bottles of Riesling wine.
examples: The tasting included several Rieslings from the same region.
They compared French and German Rieslings.
The shop stocks both dry and sweet Rieslings.

senses.definition: To thread a rope or chain through a ring, block, or hole.
examples: They rieved the rope through the block before hoisting the sail.
The cable must be rieved correctly to avoid jamming.
He carefully rieved the line through the pulley system.

senses.definition: A robber or raider, especially a historical border raider in northern England and southern Scotland.
examples: Stories of the Border rievers are part of local legend.
The village was often attacked by rievers in the past.
Ballads tell of a famous riever who stole cattle across the border.

senses.definition: Plural of riever: robbers or raiders, especially historical border raiders in northern England and southern Scotland.
examples: The castle was built to protect the area from rievers.
Songs about the rievers are still sung today.
The countryside once lived in fear of the rievers.

senses.definition: Third person singular of rieve: threads a rope or chain through a ring, block, or hole.
examples: He rieves the rope through the pulley before every climb.
The sailor rieves the line quickly and efficiently.
She rieves the cable through the guide to prevent tangling.

senses.definition: Present participle of rieve: threading a rope or chain through a ring, block, or hole.
examples: Rieving the lines took longer than expected.
They spent the morning rieving and checking the rigging.
Careless rieving can damage the rope.

senses.definition: A reduction in force; a set of job cuts in an organization, especially in American business English.
examples: The company announced a major rif last quarter.
After the rif, the department had half as many staff.
Rumors of a possible rif worried many employees.

senses.definition: Plural of rifacimento: new versions or adaptations of an existing literary or artistic work.
examples: The novel has inspired many rifacimenti over the years.
Critics compared the latest film to earlier rifacimenti of the story.
These rifacimenti show how each age reinterprets the classic text.

senses.definition: A new version, adaptation, or reworking of an existing literary or artistic work.
examples: The film is a bold rifacimento of a nineteenth-century novel.
His opera is a rifacimento of an earlier Italian work.
This modern rifacimento keeps the plot but changes the setting.

senses.definition: An antibiotic medicine, also called rifampin, used to treat serious bacterial infections such as tuberculosis.
examples: The patient was started on rifampicin for tuberculosis.
Rifampicin is usually given together with other antibiotics.
Liver function must be monitored during rifampicin treatment.

senses.definition: Different forms or doses of the antibiotic rifampicin.
examples: Various rifampicins are available in capsule and liquid form.
The study compared several rifampicins in terms of side effects.
Generic rifampicins have made treatment more affordable.

senses.definition: Another name for rifampicin, an antibiotic used to treat infections such as tuberculosis.
examples: Rifampin is a key drug in standard TB therapy.
She experienced nausea after starting rifampin.
Rifampin should not be taken with certain other medicines.

senses.definition: Different forms or doses of the antibiotic rifampin.
examples: The clinic mainly uses generic rifampins.
Researchers tested several rifampins for effectiveness.
New rifampins are being developed to fight resistance.

senses.definition: Any antibiotic of the rifamycin group, used to treat serious bacterial infections.
examples: Rifampicin is a well-known rifamycin.
The doctor chose a rifamycin for the resistant infection.
Rifamycin antibiotics are important in tuberculosis treatment.

senses.definition: Antibiotics belonging to the rifamycin group, including drugs like rifampicin.
examples: Rifamycins are widely used against mycobacterial infections.
The study compares different rifamycins.
Rifamycins can interact with many other medicines.

senses.definition: Very common, especially in a way that is bad or unpleasant.
examples: Corruption was rife in the city government.
Speculation is rife about his sudden resignation.
Disease was rife in the overcrowded camps.

senses.definition: In a way that is very common or widespread.
examples: The practice was rifely discussed in the press.
Such views are rifely held in the area.
The error occurs rifely in older reports.

senses.definition: The quality of being very common or widespread, especially in a negative way.
examples: He wrote about the rifeness of corruption in local politics.
The rifeness of disease shocked visiting doctors.
New laws aim to reduce the rifeness of fraud.

senses.definition: Plural of rifeness: different cases or types of something being very common or widespread.
examples: The report describes various rifenesses of social problems.
They studied the rifenesses of different diseases in the region.
These rifenesses pose a challenge for public health.

senses.definition: Superlative of rife: most common or widespread, especially in a bad or unpleasant way.
examples: Fraud is rifest where controls are weak.
This district is the rifest area for such crimes.
Corruption seemed rifest at the local level.

senses.definition: A short, repeated musical phrase, often played on guitar or another instrument, that forms a clear part of a song.
examples: The song opens with a catchy guitar riff.
He spent hours practicing the main riff from the track.
That riff is instantly recognizable to rock fans.

senses.definition: To play or repeat a short musical phrase, often in a relaxed or improvising way.
examples: The band riffed on the same chord for several minutes.
He likes to riff on blues patterns during rehearsals.
They riffed together until a new song emerged.

senses.definition: To talk in a lively, imaginative way, developing ideas or jokes from a simple starting point.
examples: The comedian riffed on everyday life for half an hour.
Let’s riff on that idea and see what we get.
They riffed about travel plans over coffee.

senses.definition: A large amount or style of musical riffs, especially in rock or metal music.
examples: The album is full of heavy riffage.
Fans loved the aggressive riffage on the new track.
There’s plenty of guitar riffage throughout the song.

senses.definition: Different examples or styles of musical riffage.
examples: The band explored various riffages on the record.
Their early riffages were simpler and less polished.
He experimented with new riffages in the studio.

senses.definition: Past tense of riff: played or repeated a short musical phrase, often in an improvising way.
examples: The guitarist riffed on a blues scale.
They riffed for minutes before starting the song.
He riffed quietly while the singer warmed up.

senses.definition: Past tense of riff: talked in a lively, imaginative way, developing ideas or jokes from something.
examples: The speaker riffed on the theme of change.
They riffed about their childhood memories.
She riffed on his comment and made everyone laugh.

senses.definition: A person from the Rif region of northern Morocco.
examples: He is a proud Riffian with strong ties to his homeland.
Riffians have a distinct culture and history.
Many Riffians speak both Berber and Arabic.

senses.definition: Relating to the Rif region of northern Morocco or its people.
examples: They studied Riffian traditions and customs.
Riffian music is known for its strong rhythms.
A Riffian dialect is widely spoken in the area.

senses.definition: Present participle of riff: playing or repeating short musical phrases, often in an improvising way.
examples: The guitarist was riffing wildly during the solo.
They kept riffing on the same chord progression.
Riffing like this can lead to new song ideas.

senses.definition: Present participle of riff: talking in a relaxed, imaginative way, developing ideas or jokes from something.
examples: They were riffing about future travel plans.
The host kept riffing on the guest’s funny story.
We spent the afternoon riffing on possible titles.

senses.definition: To turn over the pages of a book or pile of papers quickly and lightly.
examples: She riffled through the magazine before buying it.
He riffled the pages to find the picture.
The lawyer riffled through the documents on the desk.

senses.definition: To shuffle playing cards by dividing them into two parts and letting the cards fall together in small groups.
examples: He riffled the deck before dealing.
She riffled the cards with practiced hands.
The dealer riffled, then cut the pack.

senses.definition: A small rapid or shallow, fast-moving part of a stream.
examples: Trout often hide behind the rocks in the riffle.
The river widens and forms a gentle riffle here.
We crossed the stream at a shallow riffle.

senses.definition: Past tense of riffle: turned over pages or papers quickly and lightly.
examples: She riffled through the pages to find the recipe.
He riffled the documents without really reading them.
I riffled quickly through the report before the meeting.

senses.definition: Past tense of riffle: shuffled a pack of cards by letting two parts fall together in small groups.
examples: The dealer riffled the deck twice before dealing.
She riffled the cards and then cut them.
He riffled the pack with a loud snap.

senses.definition: A small file with curved ends, used for shaping and smoothing metal, wood, or stone in detailed work.
examples: The sculptor used a riffler to refine the statue’s features.
Jewellers often work with tiny rifflers.
He bought a new set of rifflers for model making.

senses.definition: Small curved files used in sets for detailed work on metal, wood, or stone.
examples: The kit includes several rifflers of different shapes.
Sculptors rely on rifflers for fine details.
He stored the rifflers carefully in a roll-up case.

senses.definition: Third person singular of riffle: turns over pages or papers quickly and lightly.
examples: He riffles through the report before each meeting.
She riffles the magazine while waiting.
The clerk riffles the files to find your name.

senses.definition: Third person singular of riffle: shuffles a pack of cards by letting two parts fall together in small groups.
examples: He riffles the deck before dealing each hand.
The dealer riffles the cards with great skill.
She riffles the pack twice to mix it well.

senses.definition: Plural of riffle: small rapids or shallow, fast-moving parts of a stream.
examples: The river flows over a series of riffles.
Fish often gather near the riffles.
We walked along the bank, watching the riffles sparkle.

senses.definition: Present participle of riffle: turning over pages or papers quickly and lightly.
examples: She sat riffling through the catalog.
Riffling the pages, he searched for the photograph.
He spent the afternoon riffling through old letters.

senses.definition: Present participle of riffle: shuffling a pack of cards by letting two parts fall together in small groups.
examples: The dealer sat riffling the deck.
He kept riffling the cards while he talked.
Riffling and cutting the pack, she began the game.

senses.definition: An informal term for music that features a lot of prominent guitar riffs, especially in rock.
examples: The track is pure riffola from start to finish.
If you like riffola, you’ll love this band.
The album delivers high-energy riffola throughout.

senses.definition: Songs or pieces of music that are full of strong guitar riffs.
examples: Their early hits were simple riffolas.
The setlist was packed with loud riffolas.
He prefers classic riffolas from the seventies.

senses.definition: People regarded as having low social status or bad behavior, seen as not respectable.
examples: The club refuses to let in the riffraff.
He does not want his children mixing with the local riffraff.
They tried to keep the riffraff away from the event.

senses.definition: Groups of people seen as riffraff; people regarded as having low status or bad behavior.
examples: They were treated like riffraffs by the security staff.
The manager complained about riffraffs hanging around outside.
She said she didn’t want any riffraffs at the party.

senses.definition: Plural of riff: short, repeated musical phrases in a song.
examples: The album is full of memorable riffs.
She loves rock songs with strong guitar riffs.
The riffs in this track are simple but powerful.

senses.definition: Third person singular of riff: plays or repeats a short musical phrase, often while improvising.
examples: He riffs on the main theme throughout the song.
The guitarist riffs over a simple chord progression.
She riffs freely during the solo section.

senses.definition: Third person singular of riff: talks in a lively, imaginative way, developing ideas or jokes from something.
examples: He riffs on everyday topics in his comedy routine.
The speaker riffs on audience questions.
She riffs about her travels with great energy.

senses.definition: Another form of Riffian: a person from the Rif region of northern Morocco.
examples: She married a Rifian she met while travelling.
Many Rifians now live in European cities.
The novel tells the story of a young Rifian.

senses.definition: Relating to the Rif region of northern Morocco or its people.
examples: Rifian customs differ from those of other regions.
They attended a Rifian cultural festival.
Rifian music has a strong local identity.

senses.definition: A long gun that you hold against your shoulder to fire bullets accurately.
examples: The hunter carried a rifle through the forest.
Soldiers were armed with rifles.
He cleaned his rifle after the training session.

senses.definition: To search through something quickly and roughly, often looking for something to steal.
examples: Thieves rifled the house while they were away.
Someone had rifled her handbag on the train.
The desk drawers had been rifled for valuables.
